agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to consider oversized developments in North Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to consider a public inquiry about oversized developments and their impact on neighbourhood livability in North Burnaby. The inquiry, submitted by a resident of the Capitol Hill area, raises concerns about how large new buildings affect the character and quality of life in established neighbourhoods. The resident specifically cites developments that tower over neighbouring homes, remove privacy and sunlight, and bring 8 or more vehicles into small residential areas with limited parking. Council staff will contact the writer for more information and will respond to the inquiry. No bylaw or policy change has been proposed yet — this is an early stage where Council is gathering information and deciding whether to take further action.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for updates from Burnaby Council — this inquiry could lead to future public hearings or new development rules that affect your property or business.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
1.1
Simone Riml - Oversized Developments and Neighbourhood Livability in North Burnaby
1.
Simone Riml - Oversized Developments and Neighbourhood Livability in North Burnaby.pdf
Copy

Chief Administrative Officer, General Manager Corporate Services, General Manager Planning and Development
Note from General Manager Planning and Development:
Staff in the Planning and Development Department will reach out to the writer to obtain further information, and will respond to the inquiry appropriately.

agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — June 09, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Scheduling changes at Kensington Outdoor Pool in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to change the operating schedule at Kensington Outdoor Pool. The proposed changes include removing dedicated outdoor lane swimming times and introducing a new 1:00 p.m. to 4:00 p.m. loonie swim program. Large school groups of 100–200 students are being scheduled during public swim periods, reducing usability for general public. A decision has not been made yet — this is up for discussion and a vote at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, the new schedule could affect your business if you use or rely on the pool for programs or customers.
agenda_briefs.source_body:

agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — June 09, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Daycare access, parking, and FIFA impacts at Christine Sinclair Community Centre
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider a report on proposed changes to the Christine Sinclair Community Centre that could affect local businesses. The proposal addresses daycare access, parking availability, and potential impacts from the upcoming FIFA World Cup. A resident reports that approximately 10 parking stalls are currently marked with orange cones as 24-hour reserved, and peak congestion occurs during the 4:30–6:00 pm window when daycare pickup, fitness classes, and general facility use overlap. Council will vote on whether to move forward with these changes, which may include new parking rules or adjustments to facility use. Business owners near the centre should be aware that these decisions could affect customer access and traffic patterns.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Monitor the council meeting date and consider providing input on how proposed changes could affect your business operations.
agenda_briefs.source_body:

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: BC Budget 2027 consultation — have your say
agenda_briefs.summary: The Legislative Assembly of British Columbia is inviting input on the Provincial Budget 2027. Council will consider a report on this consultation opportunity. A proposed budget sets out the province's spending and tax plans for the coming year. This is your chance to share what matters to your business before the budget is finalized.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Submit your feedback to the BC Budget 2027 consultation before the deadline — details are in the consultation paper.

agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — June 09, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Metro Vancouver wastewater rule changes for South of Fraser
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a Type 3 amendment to Metro 2050 that would identify a new Special Study Area (SSA) for the Fraser Highway Employment Lands in Langley, remove two existing SSAs (net decrease of ~4 hectares), and create a time-limited opportunity for other member jurisdictions to request new SSAs by December 31, 2027. The amendment requires a 50%+1 weighted vote by the MVRD Board.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Review the proposed SSA changes and contact Jonathan Cote (604-432-6391, jonathan.cote@metrovancouver.org) by July 10, 2026 with comments.

key_dates.label: Comment period closes for proposed Metro 2050 amendment
Deadline for member jurisdictions to request new SSAs

agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — June 09, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Central Saanich letter to Minister on housing and municipal affairs
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider sending a letter to the Ministers of Housing and Finance requesting a dedicated provincial funding stream for municipalities that have implemented housing-enabling policy changes and achieved or exceeded their housing targets. The letter specifies funding should support municipal operational capacity, neighbourhood infrastructure improvements, civil infrastructure upgrades (water, sewer, stormwater, utility capacity), and public amenities (parks, recreation facilities, community spaces).

agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — June 09, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Saanich to consider Local Government Climate Action Program funding
agenda_briefs.summary: Saanich Council passed a motion on May 11, 2026 directing the Mayor to send a letter to the Province requesting continued funding for the Local Government Climate Action Program (LGCAP). The Province's 2026/27–2028/29 Service Plan does not appear to include LGCAP renewal. Over the last three years Saanich received approximately $1.1 million from LGCAP, which was leveraged to secure approximately $1.0 million in additional external grants. The funding has supported programs including the Strata Energy Advisor Program, top-up incentives for EV Ready Plans and retrofits in multi-unit residential buildings, the Rental Apartment Retrofit Accelerator, and the Tillicum Green Infrastructure Project.
agenda_briefs.source_body:

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to consider aircraft noise over North Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a report on aircraft noise affecting North Burnaby. The report comes from the Chief Administrative Officer and several general managers. A proposed bylaw or policy change has not yet been introduced — this is an early step. Council is set to vote on whether to take further action, such as requesting changes from the airport or federal regulators. If approved, this could lead to new noise rules or operating restrictions that affect businesses in the area.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, it could lead to new noise rules affecting your business operations.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
3.5
Aircraft Noise Over North Burnaby
Copy: Chief Administrative Officer, General Manager Corporate Services, General Manager Engineering, General Manager Community Safety
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — June 09, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on Michael J. Fox Theatre closure
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposal to close the Michael J. Fox Theatre. The proposal, submitted by Blair Redlin, is listed on the upcoming council meeting agenda. If approved, the closure would affect the community's access to this performing arts venue. The decision has not yet been made, and council will consider the matter at the meeting. Business owners and community members should be aware that this is a proposed change that could impact local events, rentals, and cultural programming.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the council meeting or submit your feedback to Burnaby City Council before the vote.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — Michael J. Fox Theatre closure vote

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on rejecting Chemtrade Logistics Inc. proposal
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider a proposal to reject a plan from Chemtrade Logistics Inc. to transport chlorine through the District of North Vancouver. The agenda item, submitted by Carmen Bressanutti, asks Council to formally reject the company's proposal. A copy of the rejection proposal has been circulated to the Chief Administrative Officer and other senior city staff. This is a proposed decision only — Council has not yet voted on it.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council meeting date and attend or submit comments if you are affected by Chemtrade Logistics operations in Burnaby.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Temporary Use Permit for Southside Community Church at 7375 Kingsway
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed Temporary Use Permit (TUP) for 7375 Kingsway (Southside Community Church) to allow social service centre, religious assembly and public assembly uses for three years. Written comments must be received by 12:00 noon on May 25, 2026. The council meeting is May 26, 2026 at 5 pm at Burnaby City Hall.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Submit written comments referencing TUP #26-06 by email to legislativeservices@burnaby.ca or via Burnaby.ca/SubmitALetter by 12:00 noon on May 25, 2026.
